reasoning.name: Reasoning & Decision-Making
reasoning.description: Boros forms decisions through structured reasoning processes that transform available information into coherent, goal-directed actions.

milestones.name: Basic Decomposition

rubric.level_1: No structured reasoning. Reactive outputs with no evaluation.
rubric.level_2: Partial reasoning. Limited decomposition and weak evaluation.
rubric.level_3: Structured reasoning with multiple candidates and consistent evaluation.
rubric.level_4: Fully systematic reasoning with explicit multi-step evaluation documented in output files.

milestones.task_template: This task requires EXPLICIT structured reasoning. You MUST: (1) Call reason_decompose to break the problem into sub-problems. (2) Identify at least 2 candidate approaches and call reason_evaluate_options to score them. (3) Write a file called reasoning_trace.txt that documents your decomposition, candidates, their scores, and your final selection with justification. (4) Only then execute the chosen solution.

milestones.name: Constraint Satisfaction

rubric.level_3: Satisfies all stated constraints with documented reasoning.
rubric.level_4: Satisfies all constraints, detects implicit ones, and documents tradeoffs explicitly.

failure_modes: Mode collapse — same strategy regardless of constraints
milestones.task_template: This task has explicit constraints that MUST all be satisfied. You MUST: (1) List every constraint before starting. (2) Call reason_check_logic after forming your plan to verify no constraint is violated. (3) Document any constraint tradeoffs in a file called constraint_analysis.txt. (4) Execute only after verifying logical consistency.

milestones.name: Adaptive Multi-step Planning
anchors: Adapts strategy based on intermediate results
Detects when initial plan is failing
Recovers from partial failures without starting over

rubric.level_1: Executes a plan blindly to failure.
rubric.level_2: Stops on failure but cannot recover.
rubric.level_3: Detects failures and retries with a modified approach.
rubric.level_4: Continuously adapts plan based on tool feedback, documents adaptation decisions, and recovers efficiently.

milestones.task_template: This task will require adapting your plan as you go. You MUST: (1) Start with an explicit plan in plan.txt. (2) After each major step, check if the outcome matched expectations. (3) If it didn't, call reason_generate_plan to produce a revised plan and update plan.txt. (4) Document every plan change with a reason in plan.txt.

execution_pattern.step_4: If diverged: call reason_generate_plan, update plan.txt

rubric.level_1: No structured reasoning. Reactive outputs with no evaluation.
rubric.level_2: Partial reasoning. Limited decomposition and weak evaluation.
rubric.level_3: Structured reasoning with multiple candidates and consistent evaluation.
rubric.level_4: Fully systematic reasoning. Every decision is derived from explicit multi-step evaluation and continuously improved.

reasoning.task_template: This task requires EXPLICIT structured reasoning. You MUST follow this process before producing any output: (1) Call reason_decompose to break the problem into sub-problems. (2) Identify at least 2 candidate approaches and call reason_evaluate_options to score them against stated criteria. (3) Write a file called reasoning_trace.txt that documents your decomposition, the candidates you considered, their scores, and your final selection with justification. (4) Only then execute the chosen solution. Tasks that skip decomposition or produce output without documented reasoning will score at Level 1.

web_search.name: Web Search & Knowledge Retrieval
web_search.description: Boros retrieves external information from the web to supplement internal knowledge, focusing on identifying knowledge gaps and validating source quality.

milestones.name: Basic Retrieval

rubric.level_1: No search performed. Relies on assumed knowledge.
rubric.level_2: Searches but uses vague queries and accepts first result.
rubric.level_3: Uses targeted queries, retrieves multiple results, documents sources.
rubric.level_4: Identifies all knowledge gaps upfront, uses precise queries per gap, validates across sources, produces structured sources.txt.

failure_modes: Single vague query for complex task
milestones.task_template: This task requires active web research. You MUST NOT rely on assumed prior knowledge. Follow this process: (1) Before doing anything, identify every piece of information the task requires that you cannot verify from memory. (2) Call research_search_engine at least once per identified knowledge gap — minimum 2 separate searches total. (3) Produce a file called sources.txt listing every URL you retrieved information from. (4) Every factual claim in your output must be traceable to a source in sources.txt.

milestones.name: Multi-Source Validation

rubric.level_1: Single source per claim. No validation.
rubric.level_2: Uses 2 sources but doesn't compare or validate.
rubric.level_3: Compares across sources, notes conflicts, selects most credible.
rubric.level_4: Cross-validates all key claims across 3+ sources, explicitly notes credibility judgments, and flags uncertain information.

milestones.task_template: This task requires multi-source validation. You MUST: (1) For every key factual claim, find at least 2 independent sources. (2) Call research_search_engine with different query phrasings to find independent sources. (3) Write a file called validation_report.txt that for each claim lists: all sources found, whether they agree, and your credibility judgment. (4) Flag any claim where sources conflict.

execution_pattern.step_1: List all key claims needing validation
execution_pattern.step_2: Search with 2+ query variations per claim

milestones.name: Iterative Refinement
anchors: Detects when initial results are insufficient

rubric.level_1: Uses same query regardless of results quality.
rubric.level_2: Tries a second query if first fails, but no systematic refinement.
rubric.level_3: Systematically refines queries based on result analysis.
rubric.level_4: Applies iterative refinement loop: analyzes result gaps, reformulates queries, tracks coverage, and stops when sufficient.

failure_modes: Giving up after first search failure
milestones.task_template: This task requires iterative search refinement. You MUST: (1) Start with an initial query. (2) Analyze results — identify what's missing. (3) Formulate a refined query targeting the gap. (4) Repeat until you have sufficient coverage. (5) Write search_log.txt documenting every query tried, what it returned, and what gap it addressed. Minimum 3 search iterations required.

rubric.level_1: No effective search. Relies only on internal knowledge.
rubric.level_2: Basic search with weak queries and poor validation.
rubric.level_3: Structured search with reasonable validation and refinement.
rubric.level_4: Highly systematic retrieval. Precise queries, strong validation, and reliable knowledge extraction.

web_search.task_template: This task requires active web research. You MUST NOT rely on assumed prior knowledge. Follow this process: (1) Before doing anything, identify every piece of information the task requires that you cannot verify from memory. (2) Call research_search_engine at least once per identified knowledge gap — minimum 2 separate searches total. (3) Produce a file called sources.txt listing every URL you retrieved information from and the date accessed. (4) Every factual claim in your output must be traceable to a source in sources.txt. Tasks that skip searching or produce outputs without sourced facts will score at Level 1.
